Today, we’re introducing a smarter way to plan your PTO. With individual hotel price tracking in Google Search, you can now monitor your favorite spots and get email notifications when prices change — just like you can with flights. Here’s how it works: 

1️⃣ Head to Google Search and look up a hotel by name and add your chosen dates.

2️⃣ Tap the new price tracking toggle on.

3️⃣ Voila! You’ll get email alerts if rates change significantly during your dates. 

You can also track individual hotel prices at google.com/hotels. Just select a hotel listing and look for the price tracking toggle on the Overview or Prices tab. 

Learn more travel hacks from Google → https://goo.gle/4tdvkg9

data.text:
After 12 years as a game designer in South Korea, Hyunuk decided to hit reset on his career. Going back to study Computer Science allowed him to pivot into his current role as a Developer Advocate on the Cloud Advocacy team. “My specific journey with Google started with a summer internship at the New York office,” he explains. “That experience led me to return as a full-time Developer Advocate, initially starting at the Seattle South Lake Union office. Then, exactly a year later, I transferred to the beautiful Kirkland office, where I am today!”

Hyunuk spends his days making complex data and AI tools more approachable. By applying the same principles he used in gaming, he creates technical content, videos and step-by-step guides for products, and helps developers unlock the full potential of our tech so they feel empowered to build. “By weaving in gamification elements from my previous career, I aim to build highly engaging, interactive experiences that inspire developers,” he says.

His advice for those eyeing a pivot? "Career paths aren't linear sprints; they are marathons without a defined end. Don't compare your "behind-the-scenes" struggles with everyone else's "highlight reel" on social media, he says. “Your timeline is yours alone. It’s never too late to reset and redefine your goals.”

Want to join the Google Kirkland office? Explore our open roles → https://goo.gle/4mBD7Sr

data.text:
We’re introducing new ways for Personal Intelligence and our best image generation model, Nano Banana 2, to generate images that reflect your life, interests, and the things you care about most.

Personal Intelligence securely connects the dots across Google apps like Google Photos and Gmail so Gemini can inherently understand your likes and interests. This allows you to skip long, complex prompts, so a simple request like "design my dream house" now automatically can reflect your unique style and taste. 🏡

Gemini can even use your labeled Google Photos library of you and your loved ones as a visual guide for your creations.

Try asking Gemini things like: “Create a claymation image of me and my family enjoying our favorite activity,” and watch your inner circle come to life, automatically.

You’re always in control: This remains an opt-in experience that you can adjust in your settings at any time.

It’s rolling out now for Google AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra subscribers in the U.S., and we plan to bring this to Gemini in Chrome desktops and more users soon.

Learn more → goo.gle/4cSDCDp

data.text:
Our offices in Mountain View are home to a host of wildlife, thanks in part to our ongoing conservation efforts: monarch butterflies, honeybees, rare birds and more. But in January, the Google campus got one of its most unlikely visitors yet: a six-month-old California sea lion. Later affectionately named Babymac, the wayward pup had somehow wandered far from home and wound up in one of our parking lots.

Babymac was quickly rescued with the help of concerned Googlers, our Global Security Operations Center (GSOC) team and the Mountain View police, who kept him safe until responders from The Marine Mammal Center were able to transport him to the center for rehabilitation. He faced a long road to recovery from severe malnutrition and other maladies.

Fortunately, Babymac thrived in his treatment at The Marine Mammal Center. After graduating from a special fish smoothie mixture to eating whole herrings on his own, Babymac successfully gained the weight he needed to. In March, he passed a release exam, and was safely returned to the wild in Point Reyes, California.

“We are thrilled Babymac made such positive strides in recovery and was able to be released back to his ocean home,” says Dr. Emily Whitmer, a clinical veterinarian at The Marine Mammal Center.

As for future visits? “We always enjoy visitors at our offices at Google,” says Robert Ortiz Carlos of GSOC operational support. “But if Babymac decides to visit in the future, he’ll need to be signed in at the kiosk and escorted. Hopefully one trip to the Googleplex is enough for a lifetime.”

data.text:
Prerit’s path to Security Engineer on the Mandiant Security Validation Consulting team at Google started with a simple curiosity about how things break. While studying, he pivoted from general engineering to the specialized world of cybersecurity. "I took formal security courses and joined the hacking club, which introduced me to Capture the Flag (CTF) and the field of security engineering," he says.

That early passion led to a Google internship that moved at lightning speed. "It was my first-ever security job, and within weeks I was supported enough to co-invent a patent," he continues. 

Today, Prerit spends his day-to-day in the Google New York office keeping our clients safe. "I create and simulate malicious behaviors in controlled settings to see if my attempts are blocked or detected," he explains. "Ultimately, this helps in ensuring that the security systems are robust enough to catch and prevent real-world threats before they cause harm."

Prerit’s career advice for students: “Take initiative. Explore the different sub-niches within this vast field so you can find what interests you most. Learning the basics of how something works is the first step towards understanding how it can be hacked.”

Interested in a career like Prerit’s? Explore our open Security Engineer roles →  https://goo.gle/3ZLUg19

data.text:
When there was an opportunity to join the Cloud Business Intelligence team as a Looker Sales Lead in a new market, Rakhi made the move from India to our Melbourne Docklands office. 

Once she arrived, she found that the role was about much more than just numbers.  "Working at Google and representing Looker means more than just solving technical problems; it’s about the people. From critical customer issues to representing our tech at industry events, the energy is non-stop," she explains. “The most impactful conversations happen when we move past 'how the tool works' and start discussing how data can reshape a business model.”

To keep the creative momentum going after those big conversations, Rakhi relies on a workspace designed for spontaneous "aha!" moments. "You’ll find graffiti walls in meeting rooms, quirky Melbournian names for meeting rooms, large open space for events, table tennis/pool, photo booth and plenty of cozy breakout spaces with seating," she says about the office.

Today, Rakhi’s impact continues to grow beyond her day-to-day role. She leads initiatives for Women@Australia, and collaborates with Google Research on hearing health accessibility.

Interested in joining Rakhi and the Cloud team? Check out our open roles → https://goo.gle/4mdmcVz

data.text:
Today, we’re introducing Skills in Google Chrome, so you can build one-click workflows for your most frequently used AI prompts — like scanning long documents for important information or  generating side-by-side comparisons across multiple tabs.

When you write a prompt that you’ll want to use again, you can save it as a Skill directly from your chat history. The next time you need it, select your saved Skill in Gemini in Chrome by typing forward slash ( / ) or clicking the plus sign ( + ) button, and your Skill will run on the page you’re viewing, along with any other tabs you select.  

We’re also launching a library of ready-to-use Skills for common tasks and workflows. You’ll be able to add them as a saved Skills, and can customize them to better fit your needs by updating the prompt. 

Learn more → https://goo.gle/4cbNskD

data.text:
For Daniel, the path to his current job as Manager of Ads Marketing Analytics in Mexico City began with a passion for AI and a search for a culture where innovation is a fundamental part of the DNA.  He describes the Mexico City office as a “dream” because “it combines a world-class tech environment with the vibrant, fast-paced energy of one of the most beautiful cities in the world.”

Daniel’s day-to-day involves leading a team of data scientists and engineers to ensure every user data point serves a larger marketing purpose. His team uses “advanced analytics and cutting-edge AI to uncover key insights, optimize marketing campaigns and ensure our strategies are data-driven and highly effective.”

When it comes to joining the team, Daniel’s advice for future Googlers is to move beyond the classroom and start solving real problems. “Don’t just take classes; contribute to open-source projects, enter Kaggle competitions or solve a problem in your local community using data. Google looks for 'owners' — people who see a problem and don't wait for permission to fix it.”

Looking to join the Mexico City office? Check out our open roles → https://goo.gle/4qM2XDE

“I’m Feeling Lucky” may be a button on our Search page, but for Guru, Senior Director of Engineering in Core Data and Google Bangalore Site Lead, it’s his daily reality. “I am deeply privileged to serve this large team as one of the site leads,” he says. “Our offices in Bangalore are gorgeous, energetic and pulsing with life.”

Guru manages the infrastructure that makes AI feel seamless for billions, providing the training data and systems that enable developers to build smarter and move faster. “Our work spans from consumer, Cloud, and Enterprise, and extends across surfaces from server-side infrastructure to Android and wearables,” he explains. “All in service of creating more helpful, personalized experiences for billions of users around the world.”

Guru’s advice for success at Google? It’s all about the mindset. Those who succeed are people who are “curious, enjoy understanding how things work from the perspective of first principles, and bring patience, creativity, optimism and a sense of joy to solving incredibly difficult problems."

data.text:
Innovation is a team sport. In our Singapore office, UX Manager Kai Yee, Product Managers (PM) Lauren and Ira, and Software Engineer Janssen combined design, technical finesse and strategy to turn digital payments into a nationwide viral sensation with "Huat Pals," which allowed Google Pay users to collect digital stickers to celebrate Lunar New Year and earn rewards.

Lauren explains how the Product team transformed a utility into a cultural moment: thinking beyond transactions to create a social ecosystem. "We kind of thought about that whole game as building an economy where we would be the central bank printing out the digital currency which were these characters or digital stickers," she says.

Ira sees it as the intersection of technology, trust and everyday life. While the campaign brings joy through lovable characters and games like "Huat Rain," security remains paramount. According to Ira, "The goal is to design products that are easy and simple for users while maintaining high levels of security."

Janssen notes the project has evolved into a reusable framework for seasonal campaigns like Diwali/Deepavali in India. His role involves significant coordination with UX to ensure art and functionality align perfectly. For Janssen, the best part is a culture where everyone steps up to share expertise.

And finally, for Kai Yee, cross-functional teamwork is the driving force behind Google’s intentional product design. He explains that “UX teams partner closely with product and engineering to guarantee that experiences are engaging, well-loved and built collaboratively.”

Looking to join the collaborative environment at our Singapore office? Explore our open roles → https://goo.gle/4tS1Bdq

data.text:
Meet notebooks in the Gemini app. 📓

We’re connecting Gemini with NotebookLM, our AI-powered research partner, to create a home base for your projects that stays in sync across both tools.

Think of a notebook like a dedicated project folder for your big ideas and specific tasks. You can even move past chats into notebooks and add files like documents and PDFs for it to reference. Because Gemini draws from your specific sources, its answers are uniquely tailored to help you with the task at hand.

The best part? It all stays in sync. You can start a project in Gemini and jump into NotebookLM to use features like Video Overviews or Slide Decks to wrap things up — and vice versa.

We're rolling out notebooks in Gemini this week, starting with Google AI Ultra, Pro and Plus subscribers on the web. In the coming weeks we'll expand access to mobile, more countries across Europe and to free users.

Learn more → goo.gle/4eadmGA

data.text:
Mental health is one of the most significant public health challenges today, impacting over one billion people around the world. For many years, Google has been committed to helping people find high-quality information and crisis support in the moments they need it most. While we realize that AI tools can pose new challenges, we believe that responsible AI can play a positive role for people’s mental well-being. 

Today, we’re sharing an update on our mental health work, including some new changes to better connect people with the right information, resources, and human support at the right time on the Gemini app, including:
-Updates to Gemini to streamline the path to support for those who need it. When a conversation might signal a user may need information about mental health Gemini will surface a redesigned "Help is available" module — developed with clinical experts — to provide more effective and immediate connections to care.
-$30 million in Google.org funding globally over the next three years to help global hotlines. This funding will help effectively scale their capacity to provide immediate and safe support for people in crisis.
-As people interact with Gemini in deeper, more complex ways, our clinical, engineering and safety teams are helping Gemini respond in acute mental health situations and directing people to real-world help.
-New specific protections for minors, designed to provide the most helpful responses and avoid harmful topics when using Gemini.

Learn more about the latest updates to our mental health work → https://goo.gle/3NSkJIe

Plus, watch Laurie Richardson, VP Trust & Safety, and Dr. Megan Jones Bell, our clinical director, discussing our approach to mental health in the age of AI → https://lnkd.in/e62v9A37

data.text:
Today we're introducing Gemma 4 — our newest family of open models.

Built from the same world-class research as Gemini 3, this family of models brings breakthrough intelligence directly to your own hardware. From autonomous agents that can plan and navigate apps to local-first coding assistants, Gemma 4 is designed for the most complex reasoning tasks.

Here’s what’s new:

🔹 Longer context — up to a 256K context window to seamlessly process large datasets.

🔹 Four versatile sizes — tailored for everything from mobile phones to developer workstations.

🔹 Native multimodality — built-in support for vision and audio processing.

🔹 Global reach in 140+ languages — natively trained on over 140 languages to help you build for a worldwide audience.

🔹 Apache 2.0 license — a commercially permissive license that gives you total control to build freely and deploy securely.

By bringing our most advanced proprietary research into an open ecosystem, we’re giving you everything you need to build the next generation of AI — anywhere you want.

You can start building with Gemma 4 now in Google AI Studio, or download the model weights from Hugging Face, Kaggle, and Ollama.

Learn more → goo.gle/4s9G1yQ

data.text:
We’re rolling out two new Google Chrome features to help improve your focus: 

↕️ Organize your tabs vertically

If you’re like me and prefer working off of a large monitor, vertical tabs are perfect for freeing up some real estate at the top of your screen.

Just right-click at the top of any Chrome window and click “Show Tabs Vertically.” This moves your tabs to the side of your browser, so you can read full page titles and manage tab groups with ease — even with lots of tabs open. 

You can also pin your most important tabs for easy access. 

📖 Deepen your focus with full-page reading mode

Reading mode now has a full-page interface that reduces visual clutter and turns busy webpages into an immersive, text-focused reading experience.

You can also customize fonts, colors, text sizes and more to your liking. And if you’re multitasking, you can listen to articles instead.

Personally, I can't imagine browsing without either of these features now. Excited to hear your thoughts once you try them!

data.text:
Bryan’s career journey at Google started with the overnight shift in security. Today, he’s a licensed attorney and Global Intelligence Manager who leads one of the teams responsible for keeping our employees safe. He used Google’s Education Reimbursement Program to close the gap between where he was and where he wanted to be.

While the financial support for his degrees was a game-changer, he credits his leadership for helping him manage the balance of working full-time while finishing law school. "The support from my leadership at Google was what made the finish line possible,” he explains. “My leadership didn't just 'allow' me to study; they supported me consistently throughout the five year process."

Bryan’s advice for success at Google? "The willingness to be vulnerable to feedback,” he says. “In my experience, Googlers don’t often offer feedback to find fault, they do it because of a collective desire to produce the best possible product."

Looking for a culture that supports your long-term goals? Check out open roles → https://goo.gle/3Ebyc8z

Ask Maps lets you get answers to your complex, real-world questions with a simple conversation, and Immersive Navigation takes the guesswork out of driving with intuitive guidance. Here’s your TLDR for the biggest Maps upgrade in over a decade.

YouTube will be an official Preferred Platform for the FIFA World Cup 2026 🏆⚽

We’ll give fans a new level of access to premium content, live match highlights, and historic archives, by teaming up with official broadcasters and launching a global YouTube creator program. 

Excited to unite the world, including the next generation of sports fans, around this massive cultural moment 🌎

data.text:
“I’ve been using Search and YouTube since I got my first computer,” says Alexander, an Austin-based Googler on our Cloud Finance team. “However, it wasn’t until much later that I discovered the global reach of Google’s products.” For Alexander, that realization was the catalyst to apply to work here.  

Alexander began his journey in Genesis, our finance rotational program. Today, he works on Cloud Compliance and Assurance, where he’s currently leading a major data migration project. “In general, my work ensures Cloud business stays fast, efficient and safe,” he explains. 

Beyond his core role, Alexander also mentors students. His advice? “First: practice your interviews ahead of time so you feel comfortable without sounding robotic. Second: don’t be afraid to ask questions. You can’t learn anything until you start asking!”

Explore our open roles and career opportunities → https://goo.gle/3LY3x2Y
